Idiom (Saramaccan): Wan anu e wasi di tra anu
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Parallel verbal clause with reciprocal semantic structure
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: People help one another through cooperation and solidarity
Usage Contexts: Family life, community work, village cooperation
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects Maroon communal labor traditions along Suriname rivers
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Saramaccan): /wãn ãnu e wasi di tɾa ãnu/
First Example Sentence: Na busi, wan anu e wasi di tra anu te den e meki oso.
Second Example Sentence: Mi granma taki taki wan anu e wasi di tra anu noiti fadon.
Third Example Sentence: Den mati sori taki wan anu e wasi di tra anu na tesi ten.

Idiom (Saramaccan): Pikin faya e bron bigi oso
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Small problems can become major disasters if ignored
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Linked to historical wooden village houses vulnerable to fire
First Example Sentence: A kapiten taki pikin faya e bron bigi oso bika den no yere en.
Second Example Sentence: Te trobi bigin leki pikin sani, pikin faya e bron bigi oso.
Third Example Sentence: Mi mama e taki dati pikin faya e bron bigi oso ala ten.

Idiom (Saramaccan): Di liba no e go baka
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Time and life move forward and cannot be reversed
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Inspired by river-centered Saramaccan worldview and geography
First Example Sentence: A tata sidon pe a taki di liba no e go baka.
Second Example Sentence: Mi yere dati tori te mi granfada taki di liba no e go baka.
Third Example Sentence: Den pikin musu sabi taki di liba no e go baka.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Power or status does not guarantee wisdom
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Suma kan abi makiti ma no sabi
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Used historically to mock arrogant authority figures
First Example Sentence: Den laf te a man taki bigi ede no meki sabi.
Second Example Sentence: Mi granma no lobi suma di denki taki bigi ede meki sabi.
Third Example Sentence: Na miti, wan uman bari taki bigi ede no meki sabi.

Idiom (Saramaccan): No ala sori e abi watra na ai
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Not every visible sadness is genuine
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Sma kan meki loktu tra sma
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Common in oral storytelling and social commentary
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Saramaccan): /no ala sɔɾi e abi watɾa na ai/
First Example Sentence: Mi anti taki no ala sori e abi watra na ai te a yere di tori.
Second Example Sentence: Den no ben ferstan taki no ala sori e abi watra na ai.
Third Example Sentence: A uman ben e krin ma no ala sori e abi watra na ai.

Idiom (Saramaccan): Efu yu hari wan titei troe, a o koti
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Pushing something too far leads to damage or collapse
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Derived from rope and weaving practices in Maroon communities
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Saramaccan): /efu ju haɾi wãn titei tɾoe a o koti/
First Example Sentence: A granman taki efu yu hari wan titei troe, a o koti.
Second Example Sentence: Den ben feti langa te efu yu hari wan titei troe, a o koti.
Third Example Sentence: Mi yere en taki efu yu hari wan titei troe, a o koti na miti.

Idiom (Saramaccan): Wan bon tori e psa langa pasi
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Good news or wisdom spreads widely and quickly
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Rooted in oral transmission traditions among river communities
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Saramaccan): /wãn bɔn tɔɾi e psa laŋga pasi/
First Example Sentence: Te den win a preisi, wan bon tori e psa langa pasi.
Second Example Sentence: A nyunsu psa esi bika wan bon tori e psa langa pasi.
Third Example Sentence: Na ini foto, wan bon tori e psa langa pasi.

Idiom (Saramaccan): Baka moni no e bari pasi
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Wealth alone cannot solve every obstacle
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Moni no kan du ala sani
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Associated with traditional critiques of materialism
First Example Sentence: A tata taki baka moni no e bari pasi te a lasi en mati.
Second Example Sentence: Den sori taki baka moni no e bari pasi na ini libi.
Third Example Sentence: Mi yere dati baka moni no e bari pasi te mi ben pikin.

Idiom (Saramaccan): Di san yu sembe, na dati yu o koti
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: People eventually face consequences of their actions
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Yu o kisi baka san yu du
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Agricultural metaphor tied to planting and harvesting traditions
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Saramaccan): /di sãn ju sɛmbe na dati ju o koti/
First Example Sentence: Mi mama e taki di san yu sembe, na dati yu o koti.
Second Example Sentence: Den pikin no yere te di san yu sembe, na dati yu o koti.
Third Example Sentence: Na kerki den taki di san yu sembe, na dati yu o koti.

Idiom (Saramaccan): Sabi tide no de sabi tamara
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Knowledge and certainty can change over time
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: San yu sabi now kan seni tamara
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Traditional wisdom emphasizing humility and uncertainty
First Example Sentence: A leriman taki sabi tide no de sabi tamara.
Second Example Sentence: Mi granfada e taki sabi tide no de sabi tamara te den e feti.
Third Example Sentence: Den ede sma ben ferstan taki sabi tide no de sabi tamara.
